cases.title: 8:30 Sion game must not receive a confident verdict
cases.description: Local match 5601631110 ended at 510 seconds (8:30), well below a normal-game length. Sion (the reviewed participant) is recorded with a win and a plausible-looking partial statline, but a game this short does not contain enough evidence (no meaningful item progression, near-zero vision, single-digit CS) to support a confident performance verdict for anyone in it.

sources.description: Sanitized matches and participants rows from RuneSync history.db.

expectation.reason: A game under 900s (15 minutes) does not contain enough accumulated evidence for a confident rank/score; the reviewer or model must report insufficient_evidence rather than a confident verdict.

cases.rationale: Short/remake-length games are a known failure mode where a partial statline can look decisive by chance. The corpus and review tooling must recognize insufficient evidence rather than let a small sample produce a false-confidence score.

cases.title: K'Sante should outrank Seraphine and Vel'Koz
cases.description: Local match 5602827182 (31 minutes, a complete game) had K'Sante with first blood, two turret kills, two grub events, and a sustained lane lead on the losing team. Seraphine had a very high raw vision score but 15 deaths and no direct map event. Vel'Koz had the losing team's highest raw turret-damage figure, but only one direct structure assist and 12 deaths. The verified timeline evidence supports K'Sante over both players.

sources.description: Sanitized matches, participants, and v1 score rows from RuneSync history.db.

sub_expectations.reason: K'Sante's combat output and KDA should outrank Seraphine's high-vision, low-conversion, poor-KDA performance.
K'Sante's verified first blood, direct structure/objective events, sustained lane lead, and lower death cost should outrank Vel'Koz's raw turret-damage-heavy profile.

cases.rationale: This is the canonical disputed-score case for DAEMON Score v2: direct conversion and death cost must outweigh raw vision or turret-damage magnitude when the timeline supports that ordering.

cases.title: A frontline tank must not be penalized below a low-impact carry for low kill participation
cases.description: A synthetic (not a real captured match) tank performance: very high damage taken and mitigated, low kill/damage-to-champions numbers, but the tank enabled every won fight by soaking cooldowns and initiating.

expectation.reason: The tank's frontline/enabling role must be credited even though raw kill and damage-to-champions numbers are low.

cases.rationale: Raw damage-to-champions and KDA systematically undervalue tanks. The corpus must include a case that fails if a model scores tanks purely on those numbers.

cases.title: A low-gold, low-CS support must be scored on vision/peel/utility, not raw economy
cases.description: A synthetic support performance: minimal gold and CS by role design, but strong vision control and a high assist count relative to the team's kills.

expectation.reason: Do not require economy parity to credit a support's vision and assist-driven contribution.

cases.rationale: Naive economy-weighted scoring devalues support performances by construction. This case fails if raw gold/CS dominate the comparison.

cases.title: A split pusher must not be penalized for low kill participation given real turret pressure
cases.description: A synthetic split-push performance: heavy CS and turret damage accrued away from the team's fights, low raw kill participation, but the pressure created space that the rest of the team converted.

expectation.reason: Split push value should keep the split pusher competitive (within a small gap) even without matching kill participation; it should not be scored far below the teamfight carry.

cases.rationale: Kill-participation-heavy scoring punishes legitimate split-push strategy. This case uses a small negative min_gap to express 'must stay competitive', not 'must strictly win'.

cases.title: A weak-side player starved of resources by the game's flow must not be scored as if they played poorly
cases.description: A synthetic weak-side performance: low kill and gold numbers because the team's action was entirely on the other side of the map, not because of individual misplay.

expectation.reason: A weak-side player should not be scored dramatically below a strong-side beneficiary purely on raw kill/gold counts driven by where the game's action happened.

cases.rationale: Without map-side context, raw KDA/gold naturally penalizes weak-side players for macro conditions outside their control.

cases.title: A low-KDA player with high objective/utility influence must not be scored purely on KDA
cases.description: A synthetic performance: a poor kills/deaths/assists line but disproportionate objective damage and building pressure.

expectation.reason: Objective/building influence should partially offset a poor KDA; the low-KDA player should stay competitive rather than being scored far below a safe high-KDA player with no objective participation.

cases.rationale: KDA alone is a poor proxy for influence. This case fails if a model rewards a safe, low-participation high-KDA line far above real objective pressure.

cases.title: Very high vision score must not alone crown the top performer without conversion
cases.description: A synthetic performance: the highest vision score in the game, but no kills, minimal damage, and a losing KDA -- vision was placed but never converted into a play.

expectation.subject: SyntheticHighVisionNoConversion
expectation.reason: The highest raw vision score in the game must not by itself make this the top-ranked performance when conversion was absent.

cases.rationale: Raw vision score is easy to farm passively; it must be discounted when it did not translate into kills, objectives, or map control that mattered.

cases.title: The highest raw gold/CS in the game must not alone crown the top performer without fight influence
cases.description: A synthetic performance: the game's highest gold and CS, accumulated by farming safely without meaningful damage, objective, or kill participation.

expectation.subject: SyntheticFarmerNoInfluence
expectation.reason: The highest raw gold total in the game must not by itself make this the top-ranked performance when it was not converted into fight or objective influence.

cases.rationale: Gold and CS measure resource accumulation, not impact. This is the raw-economy companion to the vision-without-conversion case.

cases.title: Brief objective contact must not alone crown the top performer without a real contest
cases.description: A synthetic performance: a single high-damage poke onto an objective (recorded as damage_to_objectives) without ever contesting or securing it, while the rest of the team fought and lost the actual contest.

expectation.subject: SyntheticObjectivePoke
expectation.reason: Damage logged against an objective is not the same as contesting or securing it; the highest raw objective-damage figure must not alone rank this performance first.

cases.rationale: Objective damage can be inflated by poke or a Smite fight the team lost; it must be evaluated alongside whether the objective was actually secured.

cases.title: A genuinely ambiguous performance must route to manual review, not a fabricated automatic verdict
cases.description: A synthetic performance deliberately built to be ambiguous: mixed strong and weak signals with no clear majority direction (average KDA, average economy, one strong objective play, one costly death that gave up the game's only kill lead). No automatic expectation is asserted; this case exists to prove the pipeline can say 'unresolved' instead of guessing.

expectation.reason: Signals are deliberately mixed; automated evaluation must report 'no automatic verdict' rather than fabricate one.

cases.rationale: Not every case should resolve automatically. This case validates that evaluate_case() and the blinded review tool both support an honest 'insufficient consensus, needs a human' outcome.

cases.title: A deathless but only moderately influential player must not receive a near-maximal score from a spotless KDA line alone
cases.description: A synthetic (not a real captured match) performance: the player never died and posted a clean KDA, but their kill participation and objective/economy conversion were only moderate. A spotless survivability line must not by itself pin the score to the top of the 0-100 scale; that magnitude is reserved for genuinely dominant carries. This is the regression guard for the v2.1 calibration fix that pulled deathless-but-moderate performances off the score ceiling (deathless frac>=95 fell from 38.65% to 4.22% corpus-wide).

sanitized_facts.note: Deathless, clean KDA, but ~35% KP and little lead conversion -- influential enough for an above-average score, not a max-band carry.

cases.rationale: Before v2.1 the tanh score mapping saturated at ~1 robust std, so any clean deathless line pinned to ~95-98. Widening the calibration scale (multiplier 2.0) plus dominance-mined ordering labels keeps genuine spread while capping the magnitude a merely-moderate deathless game can reach.
